design_philosophy.core_loop: Traverse level platforms, defeat enemies by clubbing rather than jumping, collect bones as currency for shop upgrades
design_philosophy.primary_tension: One-hit death mechanic creates constant resource vulnerability; progression gated by bone collection and shop purchases
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Prehistoric caveman survival and exploration across hostile islands, building power through gradual upgrades
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately inverts platformer conventions—combat via melee club strikes instead of jump-on-enemies, creating a unique attack philosophy that reviewers noted as a major differentiator from genre standards

difficulty.lives_system: Limited lives with no checkpoint restart upon game over—forces complete level re-attempts
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoints exist within levels but are negated if all lives are lost; player must restart entire section
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal agency—one-hit death unless equipped with stones; stone generation from club is fixed mechanic; shop upgrades provide incremental mitigation but no difficulty selection

progression.upgrade_system: Shop-based purchasing: bones collected from defeated enemies unlock spells, ability upgrades, and items; stones enhance club attack (up to 3 stones per swing)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Bone currency gates access to shops; progression through four sequential islands (Paradise → Monster → Terror → Chaos) with multiple levels per island
replayability_hooks: Bone farming for better shop upgrades before re-attempting harder sections
Multiple stone loadout configurations via club upgrades

world_building_techniques: Island-based theming (Paradise, Monster, Terror, Chaos creates escalating environmental hostility)
Prehistoric setting with dinosaur encounters implied by design
Shop NPCs suggest civilization structures in hostile prehistoric landscape

faction_indicators: Shop proprietors represent civilization/commerce within savage islands
danger_signals: Progressive island names (Paradise → Chaos) signal escalating threat
Lava, water, pitfalls, and traps create environmental danger narratives
Single-hit death vulnerability positions player as vulnerable in hostile ecosystem
environmental_storytelling: Four island progression from safe (Paradise) to unstable (Chaos) creates implicit narrative arc
Dinosaur presence in prehistoric setting establishes historical context
Lava and water hazards emphasize environmental lethality over enemy combat

sources_used: wikipedia:Big Nose the Caveman article (describes gameplay, difficulty, progression structure, and reception)
